title: Physical and Logical Security, Identity, Authentication, and Access · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic physical and logical security, identity, authentication, and access ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in identity and least-privilege access model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Classify physical, technical, administrative, preventive, deterrent, detective, corrective, compensating, and directive controls by threat and evidence.
Separate person, device, workload, account, identifier, credential, authenticator, authentication, authorization, session, role, group, and audit event.
Compare knowledge, possession, inherence, location, behavior, certificates, tokens, biometrics, MFA, passwordless, fallback, recovery, and enrollment risks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Physical Access
steps.instruction:
Order the Physical Access CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Physical Access.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Physical Access assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Physical Access, keep Physical Access connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: People, accounts, credentials, devices, roles, badges, sessions, groups, guests, and service identities are conflated after unauthorized access. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Physical Access has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Physical Access, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Physical Access precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Physical Access, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: People, accounts, credentials, devices, roles, badges, sessions, groups, guests, and service identities are conflated after unauthorized access. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Physical Access, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A locked server-room door protects equipment from every physical and environmental threat.

options.text: Declare CompTIA A+ support user outcome, fictional ticket, asset and environment identity, authorization, hazards, accessibility and privacy needs, baseline, and stop conditions for the debug Physical Access case
Predict CompTIA A+ support reproduction, timeline, competing hypotheses, bounded test outcomes, evidence quality, escalation, and rollback triggers for the debug Physical Access case
Run the smallest deterministic CompTIA A+ support manifest review and retain the actual observation, rejected inference, next decision, and remaining uncertainty for the debug Physical Access case

Change one CompTIA A+ support user, device, version, hazard, symptom, access need, or competing cause; verify repair, rollback, original and changed cases, prevention, handoff, and controlled equipment transfer limits for the debug Physical Access case

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. The record names the assumption, applies Security Control Types, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Security Control Types record says identity and least-privilege access model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Security Control Types.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Physical Access structural check is only one gate. identity and least-privilege access model must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.
